The model favors Czech Republic at 53%, with South Africa trailing at 18%. The Czech Republic's win probability sits near the midpoint between a clear favorite and a competitive match, leaving meaningful room for an upset or stalemate.

The draw carries 29% probability, the largest single outcome after a Czech Republic win. This suggests the match has structural balance—neither team is heavily favored to dominate, and a 1–1 result or scoreless tie ranks as a plausible outcome. The 35-percentage-point gap between first and third reflects mild hierarchy rather than a mismatch.

The model and sportsbooks are tightly aligned. Czech Republic win probabilities cluster at 53–54%, and both draw estimates sit around 25–29%. The market consensus and polymarket prices show agreement within 1–2 percentage points across all three outcomes, indicating no material disagreement on the match's competitive balance.
